query: I have an existing 2-year-old Python project with a setup.py, no lockfile, and a bunch of notebooks scattered in the root. There's no src/ layout. Scaffold the ML research structure on top of it.
expected_behavior: Refuses to run the greenfield scaffold over the existing project (does NOT overwrite setup.py or create a competing pyproject.toml without warning)
Explains why scaffolding-on-top is unsafe (e.g., overwriting setup.py, breaking existing imports, dependency conflicts)
Suggests incremental adoption — at minimum pointing the user to pinning-reproducible-environments and/or enforcing-seed-hygiene as separately-applicable disciplines instead of a bulk scaffold
